one_time_description: COVID-19 assistance payments included in expenses
economic_notes: Member of Prince Albert Grand Council (12 Nations). Investments in Prince Albert First Nations Business Development LP (8.33% ownership) and Prince Albert Development Corporation (8.33% ownership). Treaty benefits specific claim initiated in 2019 against Government of Canada; $346,797 drawn on pre-settlement loan as of March 31, 2021.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2021. Province is Saskatchewan (auditor address in Saskatoon, SK). OSR is negative because total government transfers exceed total revenue due to deferral of unexpended funds reducing net revenue. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $10,767,552 + FNIHB $1,506,255 + PAGC $1,054,608 + CMHC $269,400 + First Nations Trust $243,539 + Nechapanuk $206,245 + NLCDC $26,078 = $14,073,677; osr = $13,280,403 - $14,073,677 = -$793,274. Two GBEs accounted for under modified equity method: Prince Albert First Nations Business Development LP and Prince Albert Development Corporation. One consolidated subsidiary: Section 95 Social Housing Operation. Debt covenant violation noted on one loan agreement; lender waived right to demand immediate repayment. No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text. COVID-19 assistance of $1,728,238 recorded as expense. Specific claim pre-settlement loan of $346,797 outstanding.
